Title: Motor Trend Car Road Tests: Engine Shape and Fuel Economy by Cylinder Count — 1974
Source: Motor Trend US Magazine (1974). R datasets::mtcars.
note: Among 4-cylinder cars, straight-engine cars achieve >20mpg at 100% (10/10) vs V-shape 100% (1/1). Among 8-cylinder cars, only V-shape engines exist (14/14 fail threshold). Pooled: Straight engines have 100% above-threshold rate vs 6.7% for V-shape. Cylinder count is the dominant driver — 4-cyl cars far exceed 20mpg regardless of engine shape. The association between engine shape and efficiency is entirely mediated by cylinder count.
